title: Control and Monitor User-Installed Software

checks.name: Standard Users Cannot Install Software System-Wide
checks.purpose: Verify standard users lack administrator rights and cannot install software to system directories without elevation

checks.name: Installed Software is Monitored Against Authorized List
checks.purpose: Confirm an inventory or monitoring tool compares installed software against the authorized software list and alerts on deviations

checks.name: User-Accessible Install Paths are Controlled
checks.purpose: Verify AppLocker or WDAC rules prevent execution of software installed by users in user-writable directories (e.g., AppData, Downloads, Temp)

checks.name: Standard Users Cannot Install Software System-Wide
checks.purpose: Verify standard users lack administrator rights and cannot install software to system directories without elevation

checks.name: Installed Software is Monitored Against Authorized List
checks.purpose: Confirm SCCM, Intune, or equivalent tool monitors installed software against the authorized software list and alerts on deviations

checks.name: User-Accessible Install Paths are Controlled
checks.purpose: Verify AppLocker or WDAC rules prevent execution of software from user-writable directories on the server

checks.name: Standard Users Cannot Install Packages
checks.purpose: Verify standard users lack sudo rights to package managers (apt, dpkg, yum, rpm) to prevent unauthorized package installation

checks.name: User Home Directories are Mounted with noexec
checks.purpose: Confirm /home and /tmp are mounted with the noexec option to prevent execution of user-downloaded binaries

checks.name: Installed Packages are Monitored Against Authorized Baseline
checks.purpose: Verify a process exists to compare installed packages against the authorized baseline and detect unauthorized additions

checks.name: Standard Users Cannot Install Packages
checks.purpose: Verify standard users lack sudo rights to apt or dpkg to prevent unauthorized package installation on Debian

checks.name: User Home Directories and Temp are Mounted with noexec
checks.purpose: Confirm /home and /tmp are mounted with the noexec option in /etc/fstab to prevent execution of user-downloaded binaries

checks.name: Installed Packages are Monitored Against Authorized Baseline
checks.purpose: Verify a process or tool compares the installed dpkg package list against the authorized baseline and detects unauthorized additions

remediation: Remove local admin rights from standard users. Deploy AppLocker or WDAC rules blocking execution from user-writable paths. Use SCCM or Intune to monitor software inventory against the authorized list. On Linux, restrict sudo access to package managers and mount /home and /tmp with noexec.
